name: SOAP Note
sample_text:
S: Mr. Doe is a 58-year-old male presenting with productive cough and shortness of breath x 5 days. Reports low-grade fever and pleuritic chest pain. Denies hemoptysis. ROS otherwise negative.
O: BP 138/86, HR 102, T 38.4C, RR 22, SpO2 92% on RA. Lungs: crackles in right lower lobe. Heart: tachycardic, regular. No peripheral edema.
A: Community-acquired pneumonia (J18.9), likely bacterial.
P: Chest X-ray, CBC, blood cultures. Start azithromycin 500mg PO daily x 5 days. Encourage fluids. Return if symptoms worsen.

name: Discharge Summary
sample_text:
Admission: 03/14/2026. Discharge: 03/18/2026.
Principal Diagnosis: Acute exacerbation of COPD (J44.1).
Secondary Diagnoses: Type 2 diabetes mellitus (E11.9), Essential hypertension (I10).
Procedures: None.
Hospital Course: Mrs. Smith was admitted with worsening dyspnea and productive cough. Treated with IV methylprednisolone, nebulized albuterol/ipratropium, and a 5-day course of azithromycin. Symptoms improved by day 3. Weaned to room air by day 4.
Discharge Medications: Prednisone 40mg PO daily x 5 days, albuterol inhaler 2 puffs PRN, tiotropium 18mcg inhaled daily, metformin 1000mg PO BID, lisinopril 10mg PO daily.
Follow-up: Pulmonology in 2 weeks. PCP in 1 week.
Disposition: Home with family.
Pending Results: Sputum culture pending at time of discharge.

name: H&P
sample_text:
Chief Complaint: Right-sided abdominal pain x 2 days.
HPI: 34-year-old female presenting with sharp RLQ pain that began yesterday morning. Pain is constant, 7/10, worse with movement. Associated nausea, one episode of vomiting. No diarrhea. Last meal 8 hours ago.
PMH: Asthma, GERD.
PSH: Tonsillectomy as a child.
FH: Mother with breast cancer at 62. Father with hypertension.
SH: Non-smoker, occasional alcohol, works as a teacher.
Allergies: Penicillin (hives).
Current Medications: Albuterol inhaler PRN, omeprazole 20mg PO daily.
ROS: Negative except as noted in HPI.
Physical Exam: Afebrile, vitals stable. Abdomen: tender to palpation in RLQ with rebound tenderness. Positive McBurney's sign.
A/P: Suspected acute appendicitis. Order CBC, CMP, urinalysis, abdominal CT. Surgical consult.

name: Medication Reconciliation
sample_text:
Patient: Jane Roe. MRN: 00123456.
Current medications reviewed with patient:
- Metformin 1000mg PO BID for type 2 diabetes. Continue.
- Lisinopril 20mg PO daily for hypertension. Continue.
- Atorvastatin 40mg PO QHS for hyperlipidemia. Continue.
- Aspirin 81mg PO daily for cardioprotection. Hold prior to procedure.
- Sertraline 50mg PO daily for depression. Discontinue, switching to escitalopram.
- Escitalopram 10mg PO daily. New. Start tomorrow.

name: Problem List
sample_text:
Active problems: Type 2 diabetes mellitus without complications (E11.9), onset 2018. Essential hypertension (I10), onset 2015. Hyperlipidemia (E78.5), onset 2017. Generalized anxiety disorder (F41.1), onset 2020.
Resolved: Acute bronchitis (J20.9), resolved 02/2026.
